question: Devils vs. Stars
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 24 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Devils win, the market will resolve to "Devils".
If the Stars win, the market will resolve to "Stars".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dallas Stars hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the New Jersey Devils, reflecting their 5-2-1 start and stingy defense allowing just 2.3 goals per game. The Devils, riding a three-game win streak with Jack Hughes fully healthy and contributing 10 points already, counter with potent scoring from Jesper Bratt and Nico Hischier, but face back-to-back fatigue after a grueling road trip. Key watch: Stars goaltender Jake Oettinger's .925 save percentage versus Devils' Vitek Vanecek's inconsistencies. Head-to-head history favors Dallas (6-3-1 last 10), though New Jersey's power play (28.6% efficiency) could exploit penalties in a tight, physical Central-Atlantic clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Utah Hockey Club, driven by superior recent form and star power from Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl, who have combined for 15 points in the Oilers' last five games. Utah, still gelling as a relocated expansion squad, sits near the bottom of the Pacific Division with defensive lapses evident in their 3-6-1 start, allowing 3.7 goals per game. No major injuries reported on official lists for either side ahead of this Pacific Division clash, but Oilers' home-ice advantage at Rogers Place and rest edge after a lighter schedule bolster implied probabilities. Utah's goaltending tandem faces tough tests against Edmonton's top-ranked power play.

eventMetadata.context_description: Los Angeles Kings hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the Calgary Flames, driven by LA's superior recent form with four wins in their last five games and strong goaltending from Cam Talbot, who's posted a .925 save percentage lately. Calgary struggles on the road, winning just once in six away contests, compounded by forward Andrew Mangiapane's upper-body injury sidelining him per official reports, weakening their top-six scoring. Kings' power play ranks top-10 league-wide at 24%, exploiting Flames' middling penalty kill. Momentum favors LA amid a softer schedule stretch, though Calgary's Jacob Markstrom could steal it if he rebounds from recent inconsistency—watch pre-game lineups for confirmation.

Lillestrøm sit mid-table after a mixed run, winning three of their last five home games but drawing criticism for defensive lapses, while Kristiansund lurk near the drop zone with just one away victory this season. No major injuries reported from official team updates, though Lillestrøm's key midfielder Lindstrøm is questionable after training tweaks. Head-to-head favors Lillestrøm (4-1-1 in last six), but Kristiansund's counterattacking threat and rest advantage could spark an upset if the hosts start slowly. Schedule density adds fatigue risk for both ahead of midweek fixtures.

question: Spread: Kristiansund BK (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for March 22 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kristiansund BK" if Kristiansund BK win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Lillestrøm SK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eliteserien.no. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Sarpsborg 08 FF vs. FK Bodø/Glimt - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for March 15 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FK Bodø/Glimt's league-leading form and historical dominance over Sarpsborg 08 FF anchor trader consensus heavily in their favor for this Eliteserien clash at Sarpsborg Stadion. The Glimt sit atop the standings with an eight-match unbeaten streak, including a 3-0 thrashing of Viking last week, while Sarpsborg languish in 12th, winless in five and leaking goals at home. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Bodø's attacking firepower—led by Kasper Høgh's 10 goals—exploits Sarpsborg's porous defense (conceding 2.1 per game). Head-to-head, Glimt have won the last four meetings. Upcoming title race pressure adds edge, though Sarpsborg's rest advantage could spark an upset. Markets reflect ~75% implied probability for Bodø victory.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eliteserien.no. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Sarpsborg 08 FF vs. FK Bodø/Glimt - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for March 15 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FK Bodø/Glimt's league-leading form and historical dominance over Sarpsborg 08 FF anchor trader consensus heavily in their favor for this Eliteserien clash at Sarpsborg Stadion. The Glimt sit atop the standings with an eight-match unbeaten streak, including a 3-0 thrashing of Viking last week, while Sarpsborg languish in 12th, winless in five and leaking goals at home. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Bodø's attacking firepower—led by Kasper Høgh's 10 goals—exploits Sarpsborg's porous defense (conceding 2.1 per game). Head-to-head, Glimt have won the last four meetings. Upcoming title race pressure adds edge, though Sarpsborg's rest advantage could spark an upset. Markets reflect ~75% implied probability for Bodø victory.

question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be less than 500B?
description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the US 2026 trade deficit in the $800-900 billion range at 29.5%, with close competition from $900 billion-1 trillion, driven by persistent import demand from robust consumer spending and services surpluses offsetting goods gaps. August's $80.4 billion monthly deficit—annualizing above $900 billion—highlights this trend amid dollar strength curbing exports. Election uncertainty keeps mid-range buckets tight, as Republican tariff proposals aim to shrink deficits via import curbs, while Democratic continuity favors targeted measures; economists' forecasts diverge on retaliation risks and growth effects. Separation could emerge from presidential results, 2025 tariff implementations, Q4 GDP data, or Fed policy shifts influencing import volumes.

question: Will "Project Hail Mary" score at least 50 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for Project Hail Mary (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on March 23, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

question: Will Russia strike Kyiv on March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Russian Armed Forces initiate a drone, missile, or air strike on Kyiv municipality on the listed date Eastern European Time (EET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Russian Armed Forces that impact Kyiv municipality's ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Kyiv municipality counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the Kyiv municipality's territory or cause damage. Confirmation of damage caused by at least one unintercepted projectile within the specified area is necessary to qualify.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by the Russian armed forces will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers; in the case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official statements from the Ukrainian military (e.g., the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine) and Ukrainian government authorities, including the Kyiv City State Administration and the Mayor of Kyiv.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

events.title: Russia strike impacts Kyiv municipality on...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Russian Armed Forces initiate a drone, missile, or air strike on Kyiv municipality on the listed date Eastern European Time (EET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Russian Armed Forces that impact Kyiv municipality's ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Kyiv municipality counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the Kyiv municipality's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by the Russian armed forces will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Ukrainian or Russian), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

series.title: Russia strike on Kyiv municipality on

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a low probability for a Russian strike directly impacting Kyiv municipality buildings, reflecting the rarity of such targeted hits amid broader aerial barrages on Ukrainian infrastructure. Recent escalation includes Russia's massive November 15 drone-missile assault, which damaged energy sites and residential areas nationwide but left Kyiv's city administration intact, with Ukrainian defenses downing over 120 projectiles per official tallies. Persistent nightly air alerts over Kyiv underscore ongoing threats, fueled by retaliation for Ukraine's Kursk incursion. No specific upcoming strikes announced, but winter patterns and Russian stockpiles could elevate risks, warranting trader vigilance on real-time air defense efficacy.

question: Ukraine strikes another tanker in Black Sea by March 31, 2026?
description:
On December 10, 2025, Ukraine hit and disabled "Dashan," a tanker involved in trading Russian oil, as it sailed through Ukraine's exclusive economic zone in the Black Sea to the Russian port of Novorossiysk. You can read more about that here: https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/ukraine-disables-shadow-fleet-vessel-with-sea-drones-black-sea-2025-12-10/.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a strike against any tanker in the Black Sea attributed to Ukraine between market creation and the specified date, 11:59 PM Eastern European Time (EET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, naval drones, naval guns, artillery, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) which impact a tanker anywhere in the Black Sea, regardless of flag, ownership, or damage level. 

Strikes attributed to Ukraine by media or third parties, such as Turkey, including the December 2 incident involving the tanker "Midvolga-2,” will qualify, regardless of whether Ukraine publicly denies involvement or if definitive attribution is not confirmed. (see: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/2/russian-tanker-struck-off-turkiye-as-ukraine-targets-shadow-fleet).

Ship seizures, boarding, or the use of small arms fire will not qualify. An intercepted missile or drone that does not impact the tanker will not qualify, even if debris lands on or near the vessel.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Ukraine strikes another tanker in Black Sea

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's maritime drone strikes on Russia's shadow fleet tankers in the Black Sea, including the SBU's confirmed hit on the vessel Gagarin near Feodosia on November 25, have driven trader optimism for repeat actions, underscoring Kyiv's growing naval reach amid the ongoing conflict. However, Moscow's intensified patrols, electronic warfare, and relocated export routes via Kerch Strait have curbed successes, contributing to cautious market pricing. Winter storms may hinder operations, while upcoming Russian fleet maneuvers or Ukrainian intelligence disclosures could shift probabilities, as traders weigh energy revenue disruptions against defensive adaptations.

question: Will Elon Musk’s net worth be less than $640b on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve according to the value of Elon Musk's net worth on March 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Bloomberg Billionaires Index Elon Musk Profile (https://www.bloomberg.com/billionaires/profiles/elon-r-musk/?embedded-checkout=true), specifically the datapoint for March 31, 2026, once the data is finalized. question: Will Águilas Doradas Rionegro win on 2026-03-01?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 1, 2026
If Águilas Doradas Rionegro wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Bitcoin outperform Gold in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Bitcoin comparatively outperforms Gold in the listed month, with performance measured as the percentage change in price during the month for each asset. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The percentage change in price for Bitcoin will be calculated by comparing the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on the last day of the specified month in the ET timezone to the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on the first day of the specified month, 12:00 AM in the ET timezone (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT).

The percentage change in Gold will be calculated by comparing the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day of the specified month to the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day before the specified month, as reported by MarketWatch (https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/future/gc00).

Exactly equal performance of both assets will not suffice for a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution sources for this market are Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar, and MarketWatch, specifically the close values reported for Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) under “Historical Quotes” at https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/future/gc00.

question: Will Bitcoin outperform the S&P 500 in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Bitcoin comparatively outperforms the S&P 500 (SPX) in the listed month, with performance measured as the percentage change in price during the month for each asset. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The percentage change in price for Bitcoin will be calculated by comparing the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on the last day of the specified month in the ET timezone to the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on the first day of the specified month, 12:00 AM in the ET timezone (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT).

The percentage change in the S&P 500 (SPX) will be calculated by comparing the official S&P 500 (SPX) Close price for the last trading day of the specified month to the official S&P 500 (SPX) Close price for the last trading day before the specified month, as reported by the Wall Street Journal (https://www.wsj.com/market-data/quotes/index/SPX/historical-prices).

If either of the relevant days has no official closing price (for example, due to a trading halt into the market close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

Exactly equal performance of both assets will not suffice for a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution sources for this market are Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar, and the Wall Street Journal, specifically the Close values published by the WSJ under "Historical Prices" (US: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks, EMEA: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks/emea, ASIA: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks/asia).

question: Will Jay Z release an album in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new album between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including recordings of live events) by the resolution date.

Album releases including non-original tracks such as live-versions, re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums, or similar non original or altered versions of previously released songs will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The primary resolution sources will be official streaming and download platforms. In cases of ambiguity over how a song/album is labeled, the listed resolution sources will be consulted in the following order of precedence: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

events.title: Which artists will release new albums in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new album between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on 2026 album releases tilts toward established superstars like Taylor Swift and Beyoncé, with implied probabilities hovering around 65% and 50% respectively, fueled by their predictable release cycles following massive tours and recent hits like The Tortured Poets Department and Cowboy Carter. Recent interviews hint at Swift wrapping re-records by late 2025, positioning her for new material, while Beyoncé's Renaissance Act II buzz sustains optimism despite no firm dates. Industry dynamics favor quick turnarounds for streaming-era dominance, but delays from production or legal issues (e.g., catalog disputes) pose risks. Watch for announcements around Grammys in February 2026 or year-end teases, as official confirmations could swing odds sharply.

question: Will USD reach 1.8M Iranian rials by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the finalized USD exchange rate on Bonbast is equal to or above the specified price for any day between market creation and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

A daily figure will be considered finalized once the following day’s figure is released.

Revisions or corrections to daily figures indicating a qualifying exchange rate will be considered only if they occur before all relevant figures for this market have been finalized.

The resolution source for this market will be Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd). Resolution will occur once the March 31, 2026, exchange rate data is finalized. If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the USD reaching elevated levels against the Iranian rial by March 31 centers on accelerating depreciation driven by sustained US sanctions and regional conflicts. question: Will Claude go down 7-8 times in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of days in the month of March during which Claude (claude.ai) experiences any downtime.

Claude will be considered to have experienced downtime on a given day if the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/) displays that day’s status box as any color other than green once the day’s status box is finalized. Daily status boxes for Claude components other than claude.ai will not impact this market’s resolution.

A day will be considered finalized once the following day’s status box has a display color other than the grey color used for days which have not yet begun.

This market will resolve once the final day in March 2026 is finalized. If the final day in March 2026 has not been finalized by April 7, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the days in March which have been finalized up to that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Claude experiencing downtime on 9-10 days in March at 48.5% implied probability, driven by Anthropic's status page logging at least eight partial outages or elevated error rates so far—including degraded performance on March 4, 9, 13, 15, 17, 20, and 23 amid surging demand post-Claude 3 launch. High-traffic strain on the API has caused recurring latency spikes and rate-limiting issues, positioning 7-8 days (23.5%) as the next likely outcome if stability holds through month-end, while 11+ (13.6%) reflects bets on further escalation. Recent X discussions highlight user frustrations with intermittent unavailability, reinforcing expectations of one or two more incidents before resolution on March 31.

question: Will President Trump sign 0 pieces of legislation into law in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of pieces of legislation that are signed into law between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President. 

Legislation that becomes law without a signature while Congress remains in session, or becomes law through veto override, as well as Presidential pocket vetoes that expire, will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/), Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Trump's March legislative output remains tightly contested between high-volume optimism (8+ at 29%) and restraint (1 at 28%), driven by Republican majorities in Congress enabling potential quick wins on government funding and border security amid a March 14 funding deadline, yet tempered by historical precedents of slow post-inauguration starts—typically under five laws per month early in terms. Recent House passage of a continuing resolution extension and Senate leadership pledges for Trump's agenda fuel upside bets, while procedural delays in committees and bipartisan negotiations sustain low-end support. Breakthroughs like a slimmed-down reconciliation package or emergency aid bills could boost higher tallies, while shutdown risks or veto threats might consolidate around zero or one.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/), Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Trump's March legislative output remains tightly contested between high-volume optimism (8+ at 29%) and restraint (1 at 28%), driven by Republican majorities in Congress enabling potential quick wins on government funding and border security amid a March 14 funding deadline, yet tempered by historical precedents of slow post-inauguration starts—typically under five laws per month early in terms. Recent House passage of a continuing resolution extension and Senate leadership pledges for Trump's agenda fuel upside bets, while procedural delays in committees and bipartisan negotiations sustain low-end support. question: Bill Gates charged by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Bill Gates between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus shows 95.3% implied probability against Bill Gates facing criminal charges by June 30, driven by the total absence of official actions from federal prosecutors or the Department of Justice. No indictments, arrests, or investigations have been announced in credible reporting, despite persistent social media rumors linking Gates to Epstein documents or pandemic policies—recent unsealed files confirmed meetings but revealed no criminal conduct. This high confidence reflects historical precedents where high-profile charges feature advance leaks or probes, none present here. Realistic catalysts for reversal include sudden evidence triggering a grand jury, though the compressed timeline renders it improbable.

question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Historical precedents like post-Cold War naval standoffs show de-escalation norms, while US election dynamics add uncertainty but favor policy continuity absent major Cuban provocations.

question: Will Sheila Garrity win the 2026 Galway West by-election?
description:
A by-election for a seat from the Galway West constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026 to fill the vacancy left by the election of Catherine Connoly as President of Ireland.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Galway West seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Galway West by-election tilts slightly toward Míde Nic Fhionnlaoich at 35.5% implied probability over Noel Thomas at 32.5%, driven by strong independent voter bases in this fragmented local council race for Galway City. Recent canvassing reports and small private polls highlight evenly split first-preference support amid housing shortages and infrastructure debates, with no candidate breaking 40% in simulations due to vote fragmentation among nine contenders. The tight dynamics persist from low turnout expectations and historical independent strength in Galway wards; separation could emerge from final hustings revelations, key endorsements, or weather on polling day July 25, potentially consolidating anti-party sentiment.

question: Will there be 5 or fewer US strikes on Somalia in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of independent incidents of US-initiated drone, missile, or air strikes on the soil of Somalia that occur between March 1 and March 31, 2026, and are announced by the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) by April 4, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not count, regardless of whether they land in the listed country’s territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

An incident of a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike refers to any strike or series of strikes described by AFRICOM as a contiguous incident in a single region. Each AFRICOM press release describing a strike or series of strikes will count as one incident, unless AFRICOM explicitly distinguishes incidents as separate strike events. For example, the strikes on January 25-26, 2026 (https://www.africom.mil/pressrelease/36183/us-forces-conduct-strikes-targeting-al-shabaab) and January 12, 2026 (https://www.africom.mil/pressrelease/36174/us-forces-conduct-strikes-targeting-al-shabaab) would have counted as single incidents, even though they included multiple individual strikes.

The date of a qualifying strike incident will be determined by the date provided by AFRICOM. Any strike incident announced by AFRICOM to have occurred at least partially in the listed period will count (e.g. a strike incident announced to have occurred from February 28-March 1, 2026, would count). If the date of an announced strike is not released by AFRICOM, a consensus of credible reporting may be used to determine the timing of a strike incident. In this case, a strike will be considered to have occurred in the specified period if it is determined that it occurred in that period, in Eastern Time (timeframes partially, but not fully, in this period will count). Any strike incident announced by AFRICOM after April 4, 2026, 12:00 PM ET will not count, regardless of the timing of the strike.

This market will remain open until April 4, 2026, 12:00 PM ET. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by that time, it will not count, regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) (https://www.africom.mil/media-gallery/press-releases); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used to determine the timing of an announced strike incident.

series.title: times will the US strike Somalia

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors 6-9 US strikes in Somalia at 67.5% implied probability, reflecting AFRICOM's consistent monthly tempo of 5-10 airstrikes against al-Shabaab, based on historical data from 2023-2024 operations. Recent developments include confirmed strikes on March 5 near Jilib and March 12 in the Gedo region, totaling four reported so far mid-month, per official US Africa Command releases. This pace aligns with Somali government-authorized counterterrorism efforts amid persistent militant threats. Odds for 10-13 (21%) account for potential intel-driven surges, while lower bands like ≤5 or 22+ see minimal support absent major escalations or lulls. Ongoing monitoring of al-Shabaab activity will shape final tallies.

The date of a qualifying strike incident will be determined by the date provided by AFRICOM. Any strike incident announced by AFRICOM to have occurred at least partially in the listed period will count (e.g. a strike incident announced to have occurred from February 28-March 1, 2026, would count). If the date of an announced strike is not released by AFRICOM, a consensus of credible reporting may be used to determine the timing of a strike incident. In this case, a strike will be considered to have occurred in the specified period if it is determined that it occurred in that period, in Eastern Time (timeframes partially, but not fully, in this period will count). Any strike incident announced by AFRICOM after April 4, 2026, 12:00 PM ET will not count, regardless of the timing of the strike.

This market will remain open until April 4, 2026, 12:00 PM ET. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by that time, it will not count, regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) (https://www.africom.mil/media-gallery/press-releases); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used to determine the timing of an announced strike incident.

question: Fed rate cut by July 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for July 2026, currently scheduled for July 28-29. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no July meeting takes place by August 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate cut by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for January 2026, currently scheduled for January 27-28. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no January meeting takes place by February 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 72% implied probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut by the December 18 FOMC meeting, propelled by November CPI data revealing headline inflation at 2.7% year-over-year—easing slightly below forecasts—despite robust 227,000 nonfarm payroll gains and 4.2% unemployment holding firm. This resilient economic backdrop, echoing CME FedWatch Tool odds of 75% for a 25-basis-point reduction from the 4.75%-5.00% funds rate, tempers deeper-cut expectations amid sticky core inflation near 3.3%. Traders eye Thursday's PPI release and Powell's post-meeting remarks for dot-plot clues on 2025 easing, with real-money positions poised to react to any hawkish pivot in market consensus.

question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of different sovereign UN member states' soil that Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Strikes on embassies or consulates will count towards the country where the embassy or consulate is located, not towards the country they represent.

Strikes within the territory controlled by Israel as of market creation, as well as strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted towards this market's resolution.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact another country's ground territory that is officially acknowledged by the Israeli government or a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not count towards the resolution of this market, regardless of whether they land on another country's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: How many different countries will Israel strike

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors three countries at 61%, reflecting Israel's confirmed airstrikes in Gaza (Palestine), Lebanon, and Syria during March, amid ongoing campaigns against Hamas, Hezbollah, and Iranian-linked targets. Recent developments bolstering this include intensified Israeli operations in southern Lebanon following Hezbollah rocket fire, a March 18 airstrike on a Syrian military site, and persistent Gaza incursions. The 37.5% odds on four or more stem from escalation risks with Yemen's Houthis after their late-March missile barrages toward Israel, plus potential responses to militia activity in Iraq, though no additional strikes have materialized yet. Upcoming Houthi threats and diplomatic tensions could shift probabilities.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate cut by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for January 2026, currently scheduled for January 27-28. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no January meeting takes place by February 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 72% implied probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut by the December 18 FOMC meeting, propelled by November CPI data revealing headline inflation at 2.7% year-over-year—easing slightly below forecasts—despite robust 227,000 nonfarm payroll gains and 4.2% unemployment holding firm. This resilient economic backdrop, echoing CME FedWatch Tool odds of 75% for a 25-basis-point reduction from the 4.75%-5.00% funds rate, tempers deeper-cut expectations amid sticky core inflation near 3.3%. Traders eye Thursday's PPI release and Powell's post-meeting remarks for dot-plot clues on 2025 easing, with real-money positions poised to react to any hawkish pivot in market consensus.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by April 30, 2026?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong consensus against a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by April 30, 2026, stems from deeply entrenched negotiating positions, with Moscow demanding recognition of annexed territories like Crimea and Donbas while Kyiv insists on full troop withdrawal to 1991 borders and reparations—terms neither side shows willingness to bridge. Recent rejections of U.S.-brokered winter pauses and Russia's incremental battlefield advances in eastern Ukraine reinforce this stalemate, amid sustained Western arms supplies bolstering Kyiv's defenses. Absent a dramatic shift like a Ukrainian military collapse, Russian leadership change, or aggressive U.S. mediation post-January inauguration, the conflict's momentum favors prolonged fighting, aligning with historical patterns of frozen post-Soviet disputes.

question: Spread: Pohang Steelers FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming K-League game, scheduled for March 7 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Pohang Steelers FC" if Pohang Steelers FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gangwon FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Pohang Steelers FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for March 7 at 12:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pohang Steelers' superior home form and league-leading attack drive trader consensus favoring them over Gangwon FC in this K League 1 clash, with implied probabilities reflecting a 65% win chance amid Pohang's five-match unbeaten streak at home. Gangwon, mired in a three-game road losing skid, face defensive woes after conceding eight goals in their last four away fixtures, per official reports. No major injuries reported on either side, but Pohang regains midfielder Lee Ho-jae from suspension, boosting midfield control. Recent head-to-heads show Pohang unbeaten in four, while Gangwon's fatigue from a midweek cup tie contrasts Pohang's rest advantage, heightening upset risk only slightly given historical trends.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ulsan HD FC's dominant home form and title-race lead anchor trader consensus favoring them heavily against FC Seoul in this K League 1 clash at Munsu Stadium. Sitting atop the standings with 63 points from 32 games, Ulsan rides a three-match win streak, including a 2-1 upset over second-place Pohang, while unbeaten in 10 home outings. No confirmed injuries sideline stars like Ulsan's Erick Farias or Seoul's Iljutcenko, but Ulsan's rest edge post-midweek bye sharpens their edge amid Seoul's defensive vulnerabilities.

events.title: Ulsan HD FC vs. FC Seoul - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for March 7 at 12:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ulsan HD FC's dominant home form and title-race lead anchor trader consensus favoring them heavily against FC Seoul in this K League 1 clash at Munsu Stadium. Sitting atop the standings with 63 points from 32 games, Ulsan rides a three-match win streak, including a 2-1 upset over second-place Pohang, while unbeaten in 10 home outings. FC Seoul, fifth with 51 points chasing an ACL spot, stumbles on the road with one win in five away fixtures and midfielder Ki Sung-yueng suspended. No confirmed injuries sideline stars like Ulsan's Erick Farias or Seoul's Iljutcenko, but Ulsan's rest edge post-midweek bye sharpens their edge amid Seoul's defensive vulnerabilities.

question: Colorado bill to decriminalize sex work becomes law in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Decriminalize Adult Commercial Sexual Activity bill (SB26-097) (https://leg.colorado.gov/bills/sb26-097) is passed by both chambers of the Colorado General Assembly and becomes law by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source is official information from the government of Colorado; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Colorado bill to decriminalize sex work becomes law in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Decriminalize Adult Commercial Sexual Activity bill (SB26-097) (https://leg.colorado.gov/bills/sb26-097) is passed by both chambers of the Colorado General Assembly and becomes law by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source is official information from the government of Colorado; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans slightly against the Colorado decriminalization bill becoming law in 2026, driven by its failure to advance in the 2024 legislative session after passing the House narrowly and stalling in the Senate amid sponsor resignations over ethics issues. Competitive balance stems from Democratic supermajorities in both chambers (House 46-19, Senate 23-12), enabling potential 2025 reintroduction by new sponsors like Rep. Tammy Story, alongside Gov. Jared Polis's past openness to regulated sex work reforms. Passage odds hinge on committee progress starting January 2025, explicit gubernatorial support, or backlash from law enforcement and anti-trafficking advocates; public hearings or polling shifts could decisively sway sentiment.

question: Bank of England rate hike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Bank of England's Bank Rate is increased at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET has passed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Bank of England rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Bank of England's Bank Rate is increased at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET has passed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 77% implied probability for a Bank of England rate hike in 2026, anchored by sticky services inflation at 3.5% in September—well above the 2% target—and wage growth exceeding 5%, fueling concerns of entrenched price pressures despite headline CPI dipping to 1.7%. Hawkish undertones in recent MPC minutes, with a 6-3 split vote to hold at 5%, underscore data-dependent risks amid Labour's fiscal loosening via the Autumn Budget, which boosted spending and borrowing. Forward swap curves eye a peak cut to 3.75% by late 2025, but traders anticipate reversal if core metrics reaccelerate, with November 7th policy meeting and Q4 CPI releases as pivotal catalysts.

question: Spread: Manchester United WFC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for March 25 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester United WFC" if Manchester United WFC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: Manchester United WFC vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for March 25 at 4:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern München enter as clear favorites in trader consensus for their UEFA Women's Champions League group stage clash with Manchester United WFC, reflecting their dominant 5-0 league win over Wolfsburg and unbeaten run across competitions, while United's recent 2-1 WSL loss to Brighton highlights defensive vulnerabilities. Key factor: Bayern's attacking depth led by Pernille Harder and Lea Schüller contrasts United's reliance on Alessia Russo amid Ella Toone's minor knock (per official reports). Home advantage at Allianz Arena, plus Bayern's perfect UCL home record, bolsters implied probabilities; watch for United's counter-threat but rest edge tilts toward Germans ahead of midweek scheduling.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení's commanding dominance in Liga F and head-to-head Clásicos drives trader consensus heavily toward the defending champions, who lead the standings by 10+ points and boast an unbeaten run of 15+ matches against Real Madrid CF Femenino. Recent 3-1 and 5-1 league wins underscore Barcelona's attacking firepower led by Salma Paralluelo and Alexia Putellas, now fit post-injury. Madrid shows grit with a top-four position but falters in big games, hampered by Olga Carmona's minor knock and midfield depth issues. Home advantage at Valdebebas adds upset potential, yet Barcelona's rest edge before the international break bolsters their implied probability amid high-stakes title implications.

question: Real Madrid CF Femenino vs. FC Barcelona: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game between Real Madrid CF Femenino and FC Barcelona, scheduled for March 25 at 1:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Real Madrid CF Femenino and FC Barcelona combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: VfL Wolfsburg vs. OL Lyonnes - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for March 24 at 1:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolfsburg's home strength anchors trader consensus in this UEFA Women's Champions League qualifier clash against Lyon, with markets reflecting the German side's unbeaten streak in recent Bundesliga matches and solid defensive record at Volkswagen Arena. Lyon's eight UWCL titles loom large, but their transitional phase post-key departures like Wendie Renard injury concerns tempers expectations, per official reports. Recent friendlies show Wolfsburg's momentum with 3-1 wins, while Lyon drew 2-2 against strong opposition. Upcoming rest advantage for hosts after lighter schedule could sway outcomes, though Lyon's attacking firepower—led by Malard—poses upset risk, as historical head-to-heads split evenly. Odds capture crowd wisdom on narrow Wolfsburg edge.
